channel.title: El I Chingón on I Chingon
channel.description: Recent content in El I Chingón on I Chingon

item.title: I Ching Hexagram Lookup Matrix
item.description:
How to Use the I Ching Hexagram Finder
#

This matrix allows you to quickly identify the number of any of the 64 I Ching hexagrams by crossing its two component trigrams.

  • Look for the Upper Trigram (Trigram Above) in the horizontal columns at the top.
  • Look for the Lower Trigram (Trigram Below) in the vertical rows on the left.
  • The point of intersection will yield the hexagram number according to King Wen’s traditional arrangement (後天八卦 - Hòutiān Bāguà; Later Heaven, or the Manifested World of Senses).

📌 Navigation Note: The links on each hexagram number will direct you to my Spanish translation of Richard Wilhelm’s traditional text. Within each individual article, you will also find the link to the original German source text for academic reference.

item.title: Advanced I Ching Hexagram Correspondence Table
item.description:
About this Structural Table
#

This matrix crosses King Wen’s classical ordering with its English names, Pinyin, and Simplified Chinese, along with a brief descriptor of its essence, its internal Nuclear Hexagram, and its archetypal structural correspondence in the Earlier Heaven arrangement (先天八卦 - Xiān Tiān Bāguà, or 伏羲八卦 - Fú Xī Bāguà, the binary ordering according to Emperor Fu Xi).

item.title: The I Ching and the Universe that Computes Itself
item.description: From antiquity to particle colliders and artificial intelligence, the most fundamental question persists: What is the universe? In this article, we propose a bold synthesis: the universe is not made of solid matter, but of information. The I Ching, with its binary system of Yin and Yang, anticipated this idea long ago. Today, quantum physics shows us that reality, at its most fundamental level, behaves like ‘running software’. But if the universe is code, where does consciousness fit in? Are we mere spectators, or are we the universe processing itself? We explore this paradox through the lens of Eastern philosophy (Tao Te Ching, Buddhism) and the challenges posed by modern science, such as David Chalmers’ ‘Hard Problem of Consciousness’.

item.title: The Symbolism of Holy Week Through the Lens of the I Ching
item.description: We explore how the I Ching encodes the mystery of Christ’s death and resurrection in hexagrams 23 (Disintegration), 2 (The Earth), and 24 (The Return), revealing Holy Saturday as a timeless state and the heart of both processes.

item.title: Is the I Ching the First Meme in History? 🤯
item.description: The I Ching is not just an ancient oracle, but the ancestor of all modern memes. This article explores how the 64 hexagrams align with Richard Dawkins’ theory and behave just like the ‘distracted boyfriend’ or any internet viral. An original take bridging ancient philosophy and 21st‑century digital culture.

item.title: Authenticity Verification
item.description:
In an internet saturated with AI-generated content, deepfakes, impersonation, and plagiarism, an inevitable question arises: how do you know that what you read or see truly comes from its original creator and not from an account impersonating them?

This page explains how you can cryptographically verify that any article, video, or content published on elichingon.com or any of its platforms is authentic, unaltered, and was first published by Dailingna.

Why do I cryptographically sign my content?
#

After all, “Dailingna Romero” or “El I Chingón” are unknown and relatively obscure entities on the internet (for now), but like Hexagram 20 of the I Ching (Contemplation / Guan 觀), this serves a dual purpose that reflects the very nature of the hexagram: the act of contemplating and the condition of being contemplated.

item.title: How to Consult the I Ching Using the Coin Toss Method
item.description: A practical guide to consulting the I Ching by tossing three coins. It explains the numerical value of each throw, how the 6 hexagram lines are built, the difference between fixed and moving lines, and how to identify the resulting and derived hexagrams.

item.title: License Architecture of elichingon.com
item.description:
This site is an open content ecosystem, designed as a decentralized information protocol on the I Ching. To ensure its maximum dissemination and adoption, the site’s intellectual property is structured in independent layers, each with its own legal regime.

Layer 1 · Original German Text (Public Domain)
#

The base I Ching texts used on this site correspond to the German translation by Richard Wilhelm, originally published in 1924 under the title I Ging, das Buch der Wandlungen.

item.title: The ABC of the I Ching
item.description: The I Ching is more than an oracle: it’s a universal language based on Yin and Yang. This article guides you through its binary alphabet, the 8 trigrams, and the 64 hexagrams, explaining how its words and sentences are structured.

item.title: Chance, Science, and the I Ching: A Convergence in Understanding Order and Uncertainty
item.description: Is chance a tool for knowledge? This article connects the I Ching with quantum mechanics, chaos theory, Gödel’s theorems and bootstrapping to show how chance, far from being an obstacle, rather filters our subjectivity and allows us to access deeper orders.
